title: HSI/HRI Analytic Coding Article Record
description: One coded article in the literature review corpus. See analytic-coding SKILL.md for how this is produced.

article_id.description: Stable slug, format <first_author_lastname>_<year>_<short_slug>, lowercase with underscores. Matches the filename.

source_file.description: Path or filename of the markdown article this record was coded from.

codebook_versions.description: Version of every codebook used, keyed by short codebook name (e.g. 'study_type', 'task_environment'). Add a new key whenever a new codebook is used, without changing this schema.

model.description: Model that performed the coding, if known.

notes.description: Free text for anything about the extraction process itself worth recording (e.g. partial re-code, disagreement with a prior version).

sub_attributes.description: Only present if User Study is primary or secondary.

classification.description: Keyed by '<codebook_short_name>_v<version>' (e.g. 'task_environment_v0.7'). Each value is an object whose keys are that codebook's dimension names and whose values are arrays of code assignments. New codebooks are added as new keys here without a schema change.

code.description: The assigned code, or one of the reserved values 'Not Applicable', 'Not Described', or 'Unclear / Candidate New Code'.

condition.description: Which experimental condition/sub-study this applies to, if the article has more than one and they differ.

flags.description: Every Not Applicable / Not Described / Unclear-Candidate-New-Code assignment, surfaced here so the corpus can be scanned for open review items without re-reading every classification block.
